guideline_references: GRAPPA (Group for Research and Assessment of Psoriasis and Psoriatic Arthritis) Treatment Recommendations 2021
Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology — psoriatic arthritis and the acutely hot/swollen joint
Australian guidance on the acute monoarthritis / hot swollen joint — exclusion of septic arthritis before immunosuppression
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: the presentation is alarming (sudden massive swelling of a whole finger and a knee 'full of fluid', excruciating throbbing pain preventing movement or weight-bearing, and a telephone-triage framing of 'rule out acute destructive septic arthritis before morning to prevent permanent joint damage') BUT the source note's own acute red-flag screen for joint sepsis is NEGATIVE: the involvement is ASYMMETRICAL across two entirely distinct joint types simultaneously (a small digit and a large weight-bearing joint), there is NO high-grade spiking fever (only a low-grade 37.3 C) and no rigors or systemic toxicity, and the picture carries classic psoriatic-spondyloarthritis discriminators (uniform whole-finger 'sausage' dactylitis rather than focal podagra, prominent nail pitting, established psoriasis). The in-person disposition is routine (urgent GP review in 3 days for rheumatology referral). The telehealth-correct answer is to RECOGNISE the known spondyloarthritis flare, tier T2 (prompt in-person GP review for joint examination +/- aspiration and rheumatology referral, a prescription anti-inflammatory arranged through the prescriber, elevation/rest/analgesia) and attach PROMINENT MANDATORY safety-netting for a genuine septic joint. Over-triage to ED/000 by ambulance as the baseline disposition is the scored defect; pure self-care dismissal that never arranges an in-person joint assessment is the opposite defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person home visit where the clinician measured vital signs (BP 130/80, HR 88, Temp 37.3 C, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 75 kg), palpated the finger (uniform soft-tissue swelling MCP to DIP, violaceous, tender), examined the knee (fluctuant warm effusion, restricted terminal flexion, stable cruciate/collateral ligaments) and inspected for a skin portal of entry. Breath-Ezy cannot palpate a joint, assess an effusion by examination, aspirate synovial fluid or measure vitals without a device — those clinician measurements are sealed in 10/11; the AI Doctor works from the patient's lay report and the video-observable joint appearance and nail changes.

The dramatic hot swollen joint with a low-grade fever is a deliberate red herring for acute septic arthritis, which destroys cartilage within days and cannot be definitively excluded remotely (needs joint aspiration). The discriminators here (asymmetrical two distinct joint types at once, dactylitis, nail pitting, established psoriasis, absence of high spiking fever or rigors) favour a psoriatic flare; a single monoarticular fiery-hot joint with a high swinging fever and rigors WOULD be a genuine septic-joint emergency and is built in as a mandatory escalation trigger.

Age not stated in source (Present at Visit: Patient only; lives alone; body weight 75 kg) — age_band inferred as adult and a placeholder integer age supplied because the schema requires demographics.age; flagged.
Sex not stated in source — inferred and flagged; psoriatic arthritis has no strong sex predilection, so the inference is a placeholder for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-043 (Psoriatic Arthritis Oligoarticular).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
